code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a company (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Each predicate is rendered with a short template and
    all clauses are joined with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a small formatting function
    templates = {
        "country": lambda s, o: f"based in {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"founded on {o}",
        "industry": lambda s, o: f"operates in the {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"in {o}",
        "netIncome": lambda s, o: f"net income of {o}",
        "operatingIncome": lambda s, o: f"operating income of {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"serving {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader’s title is {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"founded at {o}",
        "numberOfEmployees": lambda s, o: f"{o} employees",
        "numberOfLocations": lambda s, o: f"{o} locations",
        "service": lambda s, o: f"provides {o}",
        "type": lambda s, o: f"a {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"key person {o}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"The cost of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"located in {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"produces {o}",
        "subsidiary": lambda s, o: f"subsidiary {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies in the {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"owned by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "motto": lambda s, o: f"The motto of {s} is \"{o}\"",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} belongs to {o}",
        "areaUrban": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an urban area of {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "revenue": lambda s, o: f"revenue of {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {o}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a government type of {o}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o}% water area",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"The long name of {s} is {o}",
        "populationTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population of {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} speaks {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} sits at an elevation of {o} meters",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        fmt = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if fmt:
            clauses.append(fmt(triple.subject, triple.object))
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            clauses.append(f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}")

    if not clauses:
        return ""

    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."

    # Build a more natural complex sentence by avoiding subject repetition
    # and using appropriate relative clauses
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else ""

    # Start with the first clause (without subject repetition for subsequent parts)
    parts = [clauses[0].rstrip('.')]

    for i, clause in enumerate(clauses[1:], 1):
        # Remove trailing period from clause for processing
        clause_clean = clause.rstrip('.')

        # Extract the predicate phrase (remove subject repetition)
        clause_parts = clause_clean.split(' ', 1)
        if len(clause_parts) > 1:
            predicate_phrase = clause_parts[1]
        else:
            predicate_phrase = clause_clean

        # Choose appropriate connector based on clause type
        if predicate_phrase.startswith("is based in") or predicate_phrase.startswith("is located in") or predicate_phrase.startswith("serves the") or predicate_phrase.startswith("operates in"):
            # Use "which" for location-based clauses
            parts[-1] += f", which {predicate_phrase}"
        elif predicate_phrase.startswith("has key person") or predicate_phrase.startswith("has subsidiary") or predicate_phrase.startswith("employs") or predicate_phrase.startswith("reported") or predicate_phrase.startswith("was founded") or predicate_phrase.startswith("provides") or predicate_phrase.startswith("produces"):
            # Use "with" or "and" for additional facts
            parts[-1] += f", and {predicate_phrase}"
        elif predicate_phrase.startswith("The") or predicate_phrase.startswith("has a"):
            # Use relative clause for descriptive information
            parts[-1] += f", with {predicate_phrase.lower()}"
        else:
            # Default: use "and" for other clauses
            parts[-1] += f", and {predicate_phrase}"

    # Ensure the sentence ends with a period
    sentence = parts[-1]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
